rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JAMES MILLER SR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/30/2021 (no earlier date available). 1) The cash/non-cash allocation of the beginning assets does not match the allocation of the ending assets reported on the summary of account attached to the prior account. 2) Please explain why CALPERS pension benefits reported on Schedule A were not paid during the prior account period. 3) Please provide the amounts of other bids solicited by the petitioner, together with the names of the contractors, for the roof repairs reported on Schedule C. 4) Please provide a statement of each cash account listed on Schedule E from which the reported date-of-death cash balance can be verified. It should be noted that the personal representative's inventory and appraisal reported a different amount for the US Bank checking account. 5) No receipts have been filed for the assets distributed to the personal representative and POD beneficiaries and reported on Schedule C-2. It should be noted that some assets were reportedly distributed on 05/11/2020, before letters were issued to the personal representative. 6) Please explain what happened to the San Francisco Federal Credit Union CD account xx4065-I18. 7) According to Exhibit B, the petitioner arranged to sell 27 shares in account xx3646, but Schedule C-2 shows distribution of the shares in kind to the personal representative. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN LAWERENCE WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The sole purpose of the hearing is to determine why there has been no filed response to the Examiner's notes. Matter set for hearing to determine whether the petition should be taken off calendar. a. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.35 D.] b. If a beneficiary satisfies the requirement of Probate Code 11801, a certified copy of Letters certified within sixty (60) days and issued to the personal representative of the estate of the beneficiary, the original 13100 affidavit executed by the person(s) entitled to the beneficiary's share, or a certified copy of the Spousal/Domestic Partner Property Order issued to the beneficiary's surviving spouse/registered domestic partner must be filed before the hearing date. [LRSF 14.36 G.] c. Please explain and file receipts in support of entry 10/12/2020 Reimbursement for Pre-Sale House Expenses of $10,703.89. d. The caption of the proposed order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6)]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRIAN LANSMERE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/15/21. Issues necessitating continuance: The genealogical charts do not include the exhibit numbers for the attached exhibits. There do not appear to be exhibits supporting the parentage of the paternal aunts and uncles (confirming that they are the children of Albert L. Zusmer). Exhibit 3, p. 3, of the paternal heirs records shows the date of death of Jeannette Wood as Thirtieth August 2015, not 13 August 2015. Exhibit 6 of the paternal heirs records is illegible. Is Albert L. Zusmer the same as Louis Zausmer who is identified as the father of Hyman Zausmer on the second page of Exhibit 11 of the paternal heirs records? That Certificate of Birth, curiously, does not identify parents. Exhibit 15 of the paternal heirs records is cut off (date of birth). Exhibit 17 of the paternal heirs records shows the date of birth of David Michael as Fourth August 1956, not 1957. Exhibit 23 of the paternal heirs records shows the name as Kieran, not Kiernan. Did the maternal aunt Mary Ann Rice, who reportedly died on 26 Sep 1955 (no proof), not have any issue? Pages 2 and 3 of Exhibit 6 of the maternal heirs records is illegible. Exhibit 23 of the maternal heirs records indicates the date of birth of Joyce Valerie Badcock as Thirtieth May, 1944, not 13 May 1944. Exhibit 28, p. 2 of the maternal heirs records indicates that Frederick William John Rice's death was reported by Julie Donaghue, a daughter, but the heirship chart does not show her as one of his children. Maternal cousin Christine Ann Rice died in 1987, not 1978, according to Exhibit 32, p. 4, of the maternal heirs records. Susan Amelia Newberry's year of birth appears to be 1958, not 1959, according to Exhibit 38, p. 3. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/7/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LAVELLE HOLMES TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order Finding That Real Property Is A Trust Asset; Petition For Instructions To Resolve Questions Of Trust Construction, For An Accounting, To Surcharge Trustees For Harm To The Trust; And Complaint For Unjust Enrichment
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issues of jurisdiction and venue. The petition admits that the trustee resides and administers the trust in Maryland. The real property sought to be confirmed as an asset of the trust is located in Alabama. The petition alleges that venue is proper in San Francisco because the settlor, as former trustee prior to her death, was administering the trust and residing in San Francisco. The out-of-state trustee has not filed any pleadings that may be construed as her general appearance or consent to California courts' exercise of jurisdiction over her as trustee. The petition does not allege any facts supporting the trustee's minimum contacts with California as a basis of the Court's exercising jurisdiction. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SYED MUDDASAR HUSSAIN SHAH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The petition alleges that the Vallejo property is entirely community property on the ground that it was acquired during the marriage, even though the decedent held title as his sole and separate property. If any part of the Vallejo property was the decedent's separate property as indicated in the acquisition deed, the minor child will be entitled to succeed to 1/2 thereof, whereas the child will not be entitled to share any part determined to be community property. The petitioner filed an ex parte petition to appoint the decedent's brother as guardian ad litem with authority to disclaim any interest the minor child may have in the Vallejo property. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA DONSKOI ALEKSANDER ALEKSAND
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/13/2021 to allow court time to process new filings.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESSIE MAE RUSH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The sole purpose of the hearing is to determine whether the matter should be taken off calendar for repeated failures to file a response to the Examiner Notes. a. Notice does not indicate Lost Will. Petitions for probate of lost wills must clearly state on their face that the will is lost and both the published and mailed notice must so state. [LRSF 14.20]. b. The petition appears to need to be amended to request appointment of a person as personal representative not a law firm or file a memorandum of points and authorities in support of having a law firm as personal representative. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53]. c. Petitioner hasn't filed Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147). d. Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] e. Please amend attachment 3f(3) as it must address the factors and presumption in Probate Code 6124. f. Please provide legal authority for petitioner having priority as personal representative. g. Petitioner added a box on page 2 under #3.e. (5) Estate is insolvent (Please see Attachment 3e(5). Please provide legal basis for adding this to the judicial council form. h. The date of death must be specified for all deceased devisees and heirs. [LRSF 14.8 D. 1)]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF XIU CHAN MEI RUAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/15/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Copies of papers not file stamped will not be reviewed. [LRSF 14.7 C.] Likely due to the Court's dearth of clerks, the amended petition and related papers have not yet been filed. An amended pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] Notice may not be mailed, served, or published before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] Because of the difficulties of monitoring the issuance of Letters based on orders requiring blocked accounts, the Court discourages the use of blocked accounts on orders for probate. [LRSF 14.16 M.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/3/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LIGONG HUANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/27/2021. 1) Notice was given prior to the petition being filed. Notice must be re-done. (Probate Code 1202). 2) The Amended petition alleges all heirs at law are adults and have waived bond. No waivers are attached to the amended petition using the mandatory judicial council form DE-142. "Amended pleading" means a pleading that completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes. [CRC 7.3]. 3) Duties and Liabilities must be filed that includes petitioner's phone number. See DE-147 page 2 #2. 4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/13/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEAN FEILMOSER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/20/2021. 1) Please provide a legal basis for the Codicil being self-proving. 2) Please explain why page 2 #3.f. (3) hasn't been checked on the petition. 3) Please address Probate Code 6124. [See LRSF 14.20]. 4) On page 4 of the petition under #8 please explain Julie Manley's relationship to Decedent. 5) Please file a Duties and Liabilities that includes all required information including the telephone number. 6) Notice does not indicate lost will and proof of publication does not indicate lost will. Matter continued to allow time to re-notice and re-publish. Petitions for probate of lost wills must clearly state on their face that the will is lost and both the published and mailed notice must so state. [LRSF 14.20]. 7) A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] 8) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/7/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
